LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT RECEIVED A COPY OF THE REPLY BRIEF. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR ASSERTING HIS NEGLIGENCE PER SE CLAIMS. THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT RECEIVED A COPY OF THE REPLY BRIEF. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. DEFENDANTS LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE DEFAMATION CLAIMS ARE AT LEAST PARTIALLY BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY, THAT IS, STATEMENTS MADE TO THE EDD. (DIBLE V. HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS (2009) 170 CAL.APP.4TH 843, 850.) THE COMPLAINT AT 5 IS INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE INTO THE DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW HIS PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS ON EITHER PROTECTED OR NON-PROTECTED ACTIVITY AS THE DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT HE WILL PREVAIL ON HIS DEFAMATION CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF SUBMITS WHAT IS ALLEGED TO BE THE DECLARATION DEFENDANT DRAGUNSKAYA. (PANTO DECL. 3, EX. A.) THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO THE CONTEXT OF THIS DECLARATION: WHETHER IT WAS IN CONNECTION WITH THE EDD PROCEEDING OR WHETHER IT WAS EVER ACTUALLY PUBLISHED TO ANYONE. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO OTHER EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES IS DENIED. IF DEFENDANTS SEEK ATTORNEYS FEES, THEY CAN MAKE A MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To May-29-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alexei Kuchinsky, Esq. of the Klein Law Group, Tel. 415.693.9107, for defendant and moving party;
Arcolina Panto, Esq. of the Law Offices of Arcolina Panto, Tel. 510.658.2500, for plaintiff.

RULING: The Court received a copy of the reply brief. The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first cause of action for negligence per se and overruled as to the remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must allege the statutory basis for asserting his negligence per se claims. The remaining causes of action are adequately pled. The allegations are not uncertain. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alexei Kuchinsky, Esq. of the Klein Law Group, Tel. 415.693.9107, for defendant and moving party;
Arcolina Panto, Esq. of the Law Offices of Arcolina Panto, Tel. 510.658.2500, for plaintiff.

RULING: The Court received a copy of the reply brief. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. DEFENDANTs LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'s SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is Granted. Defendants have met their burden to show that the defamation claims are at least partially based on protected activity, that is, statements made to the EDD. (Dible v. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 843, 850.) The Complaint at 5 is incorporated by reference into the defamation cause of action. The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show his probability of prevailing on the merits on either protected or non-protected activity as the defamation cause of action. Plaintiff has not provided sufficient evidence to show that he will prevail on his defamation claims. Plaintiff submits what is alleged to be the declaration Defendant Dragunskaya. (Panto Decl. 3, Ex. A.) There is no evidence as to the context of this declaration: whether it was in connection with the EDD proceeding or whether it was ever actually published to anyone. Plaintiff has presented no other evidence to establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. Request for attorneys fees is denied. If defendants seek attorneys fees, they can make a motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
